name: smallest_bin_fitting_size_roundtrip
reason: If the smallest bin fitting a size couldn't actually hold that size, the allocator would return undersized blocks

name: pfd_lower_upper_coverage
reason: If lower bound of bin N exceeds upper bound of bin N-1, there's a gap — word sizes between bins would have no valid bin, causing allocation failures

name: size_of_bin_monotone
reason: If bin sizes weren't monotonically increasing, the binary-search-like logic in smallest_bin_fitting_size would return incorrect bins

name: size_of_bin_divisible_by_8
reason: All bin sizes should be word-aligned (divisible by 8) — if not, allocations would return misaligned blocks causing undefined behavior on architectures requiring alignment

name: pfd_lower_leq_upper
reason: If the lower bound of a bin exceeded its upper bound, the bin would represent an empty range of word sizes — no allocation could ever use that bin
